verses.latin_vulgate: Filius viginti et unius anni erat Sedecias cum regnare cœpisset, et undecim annis regnavit in Jerusalem. Et nomen matris ejus Amital filia Jeremiæ de Lobna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֶּן־עֶשְׂרִ֨ים וְאַחַ֤ת שָׁנָה֙ צִדְקִיָּ֣הוּ בְמׇלְכ֔וֹ וְאַחַ֤ת עֶשְׂרֵה֙ שָׁנָ֔ה מָלַ֖ךְ בִּירוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וְשֵׁ֣ם אִמּ֔וֹ (חמיטל) [חֲמוּטַ֥ל] בַּֽת־יִרְמְיָ֖הוּ מִלִּבְנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Sedecias was one and twenty years old when he began to reign: and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem: and the name of his mother was Amital, the daughter of Jeremiah of Lobna.
verses.finnish_biblia: Zedekia oli yhdenkolmattakymmentä vuoden vanha tullessa kuninkaaksi, ja hallitsi yksitoistakymmentä vuotta Jerusalemissa. Hänen äitinsä nimi oli Hamutal Jeremian tytär Libnasta.
verses.king_james: Zedekiah was one and twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fecit malum in oculis Domini, juxta omnia quæ fecerat Joakim,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַשׂ הָרַ֖ע בְּעֵינֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה כְּכֹ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֖ה יְהוֹיָקִֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he did that which was evil in the eyes of the Lord, according to all that Joakim had done.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän teki pahaa Herran edessä, niinkuin Jojakimkin oli tehnyt.
verses.king_james: And he did that which was evil in the eyes of the Lord, according to all that Jehoiakim had done.

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam furor Domini erat in Jerusalem et in Juda, usquequo projiceret eos a facie sua : et recessit Sedecias a rege Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י ׀ עַל־אַ֣ף יְהֹוָ֗ה הָֽיְתָה֙ בִּירוּשָׁלַ֣͏ִם וִֽיהוּדָ֔ה עַד־הִשְׁלִיכ֥וֹ אוֹתָ֖ם מֵעַ֣ל פָּנָ֑יו וַיִּמְרֹ֥ד צִדְקִיָּ֖הוּ בְּמֶ֥לֶךְ בָּבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the wrath of the Lord was against Jerusalem, and against Juda, till he cast them out from his presence: and Sedecias revolted from the king of Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herran viha oli Jerusalemin ja Juudan päällä, siihenasti että hän heitti heidät pois kasvoinsa edestä. Ja Zedekia luopui Babelin kuninkaasta.
verses.king_james: For through the anger of the Lord it came to pass in Jerusalem and Judah, till he had cast them out from his presence, that Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem in anno nono regni ejus, in mense decimo, decima mensis, venit Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis, ipse et omnis exercitus ejus, adversus Jerusalem : et obsederunt eam, et ædificaverunt contra eam munitiones in circuitu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֩ בַשָּׁנָ֨ה הַתְּשִׁעִ֜ית לְמׇלְכ֗וֹ בַּחֹ֣דֶשׁ הָעֲשִׂירִי֮ בֶּעָשׂ֣וֹר לַחֹ֒דֶשׁ֒ בָּ֠א נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֨ר מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֜ל ה֤וּא וְכׇל־חֵילוֹ֙ עַל־יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וַֽיַּחֲנ֖וּ עָלֶ֑יהָ וַיִּבְנ֥וּ עָלֶ֛יהָ דָּיֵ֖ק סָבִֽיב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, the tenth day of the month, that Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem, and they besieged it, and built forts against it round about.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui hänen valtakuntansa yhdeksäntenä vuotena, kymmenentenä päivänä, kymmenennessä kuukaudessa, että Nebukadnetsar, Babelin kuningas tuli kaiken joukkonsa kanssa Jerusalemia vastaan; ja he sioittuivat sen eteen, ja rakensivat saarron sen ympärille joka taholta.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, in the tenth day of the month, that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem, and pitched against it, and built forts against it round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fuit civitas obsessa usque ad undecimum annum regis Sedeciæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּבֹ֥א הָעִ֖יר בַּמָּצ֑וֹר עַ֚ד עַשְׁתֵּ֣י עֶשְׂרֵ֣ה שָׁנָ֔ה לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ צִדְקִיָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the city was besieged until the eleventh year of king Sedecias.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaupunki oli niin piiritetty hamaan yhteentoistakymmenenteen kuningas Zedekian vuoteen asti.
verses.king_james: So the city was besieged unto the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mense autem quarto, nona mensis, obtinuit fames civitatem, et non erant alimenta populo terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּחֹ֤דֶשׁ הָרְבִיעִי֙ בְּתִשְׁעָ֣ה לַחֹ֔דֶשׁ וַיֶּחֱזַ֥ק הָרָעָ֖ב בָּעִ֑יר וְלֹא־הָיָ֥ה לֶ֖חֶם לְעַ֥ם הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And in the fourth month, the ninth day of the month, a famine overpowered the city: and there was no food for the people of the land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Yhdeksäntenä päivänä neljännessä kuukaudessa sai nälkä vallan kaupungissa, ja ei ollut maakunnan kansalla enään mitään syötävää.
verses.king_james: And in the fourth month, in the ninth day of the month, the famine was sore in the city, so that there was no bread for the people of the land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dirupta est civitas, et omnes viri bellatores ejus fugerunt, exieruntque de civitate nocte, per viam portæ quæ est inter duos muros, et ducit ad hortum regis, Chaldæis obsidentibus urbem in gyro, et abierunt per viam quæ ducit in eremum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּבָּקַ֣ע הָעִ֗יר וְכׇל־אַנְשֵׁ֣י הַמִּלְחָמָ֡ה יִבְרְחוּ֩ וַיֵּצְא֨וּ מֵהָעִ֜יר לַ֗יְלָה דֶּ֜רֶךְ שַׁ֤עַר בֵּין־הַחֹמֹתַ֙יִם֙ אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל־גַּ֣ן הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וְכַשְׂדִּ֥ים עַל־הָעִ֖יר סָבִ֑יב וַיֵּלְכ֖וּ דֶּ֥רֶךְ הָעֲרָבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the city was broken up, and the men of war fled, and went out of the city in the night by the way of the gate that is between the two walls, and leadeth to the king's garden, (the Chaldeans besieging the city round about,) and they went by the way that leadeth to the wilderness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kukistettiin kaupunki, ja kaikki sotamiehet pakenivat ja menivät ulos kaupungista, yöllä, porttitietä, joka oli kahden muurin välillä kuninkaan yrttitarhaa kohden; mutta Kaldealaiset makasivat kaupungin ympärillä, ja nämät menivät pois tietänsä myöten kedolle.
verses.king_james: Then the city was broken up, and all the men of war fled, and went forth out of the city by night by the way of the gate between the two walls, which was by the king’s garden; (now the Chaldeans were by the city round about:) and they went by the way of the plain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Persecutus est autem Chadæorum exercitus regem, et apprehenderunt Sedeciam in deserto quod est juxta Jericho : et omnis comitatus ejus diffugit ab eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְדְּפ֤וּ חֵיל־כַּשְׂדִּים֙ אַחֲרֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַיַּשִּׂ֥יגוּ אֶת־צִדְקִיָּ֖הוּ בְּעַֽרְבֹ֣ת יְרֵח֑וֹ וְכׇ֨ל־חֵיל֔וֹ נָפֹ֖צוּ מֵעָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king: and they overtook Sedecias in the desert which is near Jericho: and all his companions were scattered from him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ajoi Kaldealaisten sotajoukko kuningasta takaa, ja saavutti Zedekian Jerikon kedolla, ja kaikki hänen sotajoukkonsa oli hänestä hajonnut.
verses.king_james: But the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque comprehendissent regem, adduxerunt eum ad regem Babylonis in Reblatha, quæ est in terra Emath, et locutus est ad eum judicia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיִּתְפְּשׂוּ֙ אֶת־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַיַּעֲל֨וּ אֹת֜וֹ אֶל־מֶ֧לֶךְ בָּבֶ֛ל רִבְלָ֖תָה בְּאֶ֣רֶץ חֲמָ֑ת וַיְדַבֵּ֥ר אִתּ֖וֹ מִשְׁפָּטִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they had taken the king, they carried him to the king of Babylon to Reblatha, which is in the land of Emath: and he gave judgment upon him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he ottivat kuninkaan kiinni ja veivät hänet Babelin kuninkaan tykö Riblatiin, joka on Hamatin maalla, ja hän sanoi tuomion hänestä.
verses.king_james: Then they took the king, and carried him up unto the king of Babylon to Riblah in the land of Hamath; where he gave judgment upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et jugulavit rex Babylonis filios Sedeciæ in oculis ejus, sed et omnes principes Juda occidit in Reblatha.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁחַ֧ט מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֛ל אֶת־בְּנֵ֥י צִדְקִיָּ֖הוּ לְעֵינָ֑יו וְגַ֛ם אֶת־כׇּל־שָׂרֵ֥י יְהוּדָ֖ה שָׁחַ֥ט בְּרִבְלָֽתָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Sedecias before his eyes: and he slew all the princes of Juda in Reblatha.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Babelin kuningas antoi tappaa Zedekian lapset, hänen silmäinsä edessä; ja tappoi kaikki Juudan ruhtinaat Riblatissa.
verses.king_james: And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes: he slew also all the princes of Judah in Riblah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et oculos Sedeciæ eruit, et vinxit eum compedibus, et adduxit eum rex Babylonis in Babylonem, et posuit eum in domo carceris usque ad diem mortis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־עֵינֵ֥י צִדְקִיָּ֖הוּ עִוֵּ֑ר וַיַּאַסְרֵ֣הוּ בַֽנְחֻשְׁתַּ֗יִם וַיְבִאֵ֤הוּ מֶֽלֶךְ־בָּבֶל֙ בָּבֶ֔לָה וַיִּתְּנֵ֥הוּ (בבית) [בֵֽית־]הַפְּקֻדֹּ֖ת עַד־י֥וֹם מוֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he put out the eyes of Sedecias, and bound him with fetters, and the king of Babylon brought him into Babylon, and he put him in prison till the day of his death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Zedekialta hän antoi puhkaista silmät, ja antoi hänen sidottaa kaksilla vaskikahleilla; ja Babelin kuningas vei hänen Babeliin, ja hän pani hänen vankihuoneesen, siihenasti kuin hän kuoli.
verses.king_james: Then he put out the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him in chains, and carried him to Babylon, and put him in prison till the day of his death.

verses.latin_vulgate: In mense autem quinto, decima mensis, ipse est annus nonusdecimus Nabuchodonosor regis Babylonis, venit Nabuzardan princeps militiæ, qui stabat coram rege Babylonis, in Jerusalem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבַחֹ֤דֶשׁ הַֽחֲמִישִׁי֙ בֶּעָשׂ֣וֹר לַחֹ֔דֶשׁ הִ֗יא שְׁנַת֙ תְּשַֽׁע־עֶשְׂרֵ֣ה שָׁנָ֔ה לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֣ר מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֑ל בָּ֗א נְבֽוּזַרְאֲדָן֙ רַב־טַבָּחִ֔ים עָמַ֛ד לִפְנֵ֥י מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֖ל בִּירוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And in the fifth month, the tenth day of the month, the same is the nineteenth year of Nabuchodonosor, king of Babylon, came Nabuzardan the general of the army, who stood before the king of Babylon in Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kymmenentenä päivänä, viidentenä kuukautena, joka oli Nebukadnetsarin, Babelin kuninkaan yhdeksästoistakymmenes vuosi, tuli Nebusaradan, huovinhaltia, joka seisoi Babelin kuninkaan edessä, Jerusalemiin.
verses.king_james: Now in the fifth month, in the tenth day of the month, which was the nineteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, came Nebuzar–adan, captain of the guard, which served the king of Babylon, into Jerusalem,

verses.latin_vulgate: et incendit domum Domini, et domum regis, et omnes domos Jerusalem : et omnem domum magnam igni combussit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׂרֹ֥ף אֶת־בֵּית־יְהֹוָ֖ה וְאֶת־בֵּ֣ית הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וְאֵ֨ת כׇּל־בָּתֵּ֧י יְרוּשָׁלַ֛͏ִם וְאֶת־כׇּל־בֵּ֥ית הַגָּד֖וֹל שָׂרַ֥ף בָּאֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he burnt the house of the Lord, and the king's house, and all the houses of Jerusalem, and every great house he burnt with fire.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja poltti Herran huoneen ja kuninkaan huoneen, ja kaikki huoneet Jerusalemissa; kaikki suuret huoneet poltti hän tulella.
verses.king_james: And burned the house of the Lord, and the king’s house; and all the houses of Jerusalem, and all the houses of the great men, burned he with fire:

verses.latin_vulgate: et totum murum Jerusalem per circuitum destruxit cunctus exercitus Chaldæorum qui erat cum magistro militiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־כׇּל־חֹמ֥וֹת יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם סָבִ֑יב נָֽתְצוּ֙ כׇּל־חֵ֣יל כַּשְׂדִּ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֖ר אֶת־רַב־טַבָּחִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the army of the Chaldeans that were with the general broke down all the wall of Jerusalem round about.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki Kaldealaisten sotajoukko, joka huovinhaltian kanssa oli, jaotti kaikki muurit Jerusalemissa joka taholta ympärinsä.
verses.king_james: And all the army of the Chaldeans, that were with the captain of the guard, brake down all the walls of Jerusalem round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: De pauperibus autem populi, et de reliquo vulgo quod remanserat in civitate, et de perfugis qui transfugerant ad regem Babylonis, et ceteros de multitudine transtulit Nabuzardan princeps militiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִדַּלּ֨וֹת הָעָ֜ם וְֽאֶת־יֶ֥תֶר הָעָ֣ם ׀ הַנִּשְׁאָרִ֣ים בָּעִ֗יר וְאֶת־הַנֹּֽפְלִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֤ר נָֽפְלוּ֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ בָּבֶ֔ל וְאֵ֖ת יֶ֣תֶר הָאָמ֑וֹן הֶגְלָ֕ה נְבוּזַרְאֲדָ֖ן רַב־טַבָּחִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Nabuzardan the general carried away captives some of the poor people, and of the rest of the common sort who remained in the city, and of the fugitives that were fled over to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the multitude.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta köyhän väen ja muun kansan, joka oli vielä jäänyt kaupunkiin, ja ne, jotka olivat itsensä antaneet Babelin kuninkaan alle, ja jääneet kansasta, vei Nebusaradan, huovinhaltia, vangittuna pois.
verses.king_james: Then Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard carried away captive certain of the poor of the people, and the residue of the people that remained in the city, and those that fell away, that fell to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the multitude.

verses.latin_vulgate: De pauperibus vero terræ reliquit Nabuzardan princeps militiæ vinitores et agricolas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִדַּלּ֣וֹת הָאָ֔רֶץ הִשְׁאִ֕יר נְבוּזַרְאֲדָ֖ן רַב־טַבָּחִ֑ים לְכֹרְמִ֖ים וּלְיֹגְבִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: But of the poor of the land, Nabuzardan the general left some for vinedressers, and for husbandmen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Nebusaradan, huovinhaltia, antoi monikahdat köyhästä maan väestä jäädä viinamäkien ja peltoin rakentajiksi.
verses.king_james: But Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard left certain of the poor of the land for vinedressers and for husbandmen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Columnas quoque æreas quæ erant in domo Domini, et bases, et mare æneum quod erat in domo Domini, confregerunt Chaldæi, et tulerunt omne æs eorum in Babylonem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־עַמּוּדֵ֨י הַנְּחֹ֜שֶׁת אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְבֵית־יְהֹוָ֗ה וְֽאֶת־הַמְּכֹנ֞וֹת וְאֶת־יָ֧ם הַנְּחֹ֛שֶׁת אֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּבֵית־יְהֹוָ֖ה שִׁבְּר֣וּ כַשְׂדִּ֑ים וַיִּשְׂא֥וּ אֶת־כׇּל־נְחֻשְׁתָּ֖ם בָּבֶֽלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Chaldeans also broke in pieces the brazen pillars that were in the house of the Lord, and the bases, and the sea of brass that was in the house of the Lord: and they carried all the brass of them to Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta vaskipatsaat, jotka olivat Herran huoneessa, ja istuimet ja vaskimeren, joka Herran huoneessa oli, rikkoivat Kaldealaiset, ja veivät kaiken vasken Babeliin.
verses.king_james: Also the pillars of brass that were in the house of the Lord, and the bases, and the brasen sea that was in the house of the Lord, the Chaldeans brake, and carried all the brass of them to Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: et lebetes, et creagras, et psalteria, et phialas, et mortariola, et omnia vasa ærea quæ in ministerio fuerant, tulerunt :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־הַ֠סִּר֠וֹת וְאֶת־הַיָּעִ֨ים וְאֶת־הַֽמְזַמְּר֜וֹת וְאֶת־הַמִּזְרָקֹ֣ת וְאֶת־הַכַּפּ֗וֹת וְאֵ֨ת כׇּל־כְּלֵ֧י הַנְּחֹ֛שֶׁת אֲשֶׁר־יְשָׁרְת֥וּ בָהֶ֖ם לָקָֽחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they took the caldrons, and the fleshhooks, and the psalteries, and the bowls, and the little mortars, and all the brazen vessels that had been used in the ministry: and
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kattilat, lapiot, veitset, vaskimaljat, lusikat ja kaikki vaski-astiat, joilla palveltiin, ottivat he pois.
verses.king_james: The caldrons also, and the shovels, and the snuffers, and the bowls, and the spoons, and all the vessels of brass wherewith they ministered, took they away.

verses.latin_vulgate: et hydrias, et thymiamateria, et urceos, et pelves, et candelabra, et mortaria, et cyathos, quotquot aurea, aurea, et quotquot argentea, argentea, tulit magister militiæ :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־הַ֠סִּפִּ֠ים וְאֶת־הַמַּחְתּ֨וֹת וְאֶת־הַמִּזְרָק֜וֹת וְאֶת־הַסִּיר֣וֹת וְאֶת־הַמְּנֹר֗וֹת וְאֶת־הַכַּפּוֹת֙ וְאֶת־הַמְּנַקִּיּ֔וֹת אֲשֶׁ֤ר זָהָב֙ זָהָ֔ב וַאֲשֶׁר־כֶּ֖סֶף כָּ֑סֶף לָקַ֖ח רַב־טַבָּחִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: The general took away the pitchers, and the censers, and the pots, and the basins, and the candlesticks, and the mortars, and the cups: as many as were of gold, in gold: and as many as were of silver, in silver:
verses.finnish_biblia: Otti myös huovinhaltia pois kaikki, mitkä kullasta ja hopeasta oli, juoma-astiat, savi-astiat, maljat, kattilat, kynttiläjalat, lusikat ja kupit.
verses.king_james: And the basons, and the firepans, and the bowls, and the caldrons, and the candlesticks, and the spoons, and the cups; that which was of gold in gold, and that which was of silver in silver, took the captain of the guard away.

verses.latin_vulgate: et columnas duas, et mare unum, et vitulos duodecim æreos qui erant sub basibus quas fecerat rex Salomon in domo Domini. Non erat pondus æris omnium horum vasorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָעַמּוּדִ֣ים ׀ שְׁנַ֗יִם הַיָּ֤ם אֶחָד֙ וְהַבָּקָ֞ר שְׁנֵים־עָשָׂ֤ר נְחֹ֙שֶׁת֙ אֲשֶׁר־תַּ֣חַת הַמְּכֹנ֔וֹת אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָׂ֛ה הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֖ה לְבֵ֣ית יְהֹוָ֑ה לֹא־הָיָ֣ה מִשְׁקָ֔ל לִנְחֻשְׁתָּ֖ם כׇּל־הַכֵּלִ֥ים הָאֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the two pillars, and one sea, and twelve oxen of brass that were under the bases, which king Solomon had made in the house of the Lord: there was no weight of the brass of all these vessels.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ne kaksi patsasta, sen yhden meren, ne kaksitoistakymmentä vaskihärkää, jotka seisoivat jalkain alla, jotka kuningas Salomo oli antanut tehdä Herran huoneesen, näistä astioista karttui ylönpalttinen vaski.
verses.king_james: The two pillars, one sea, and twelve brasen bulls that were under the bases, which king Solomon had made in the house of the Lord: the brass of all these vessels was without weight.

verses.latin_vulgate: De columnis autem decem et octo cubiti altitudinis erant in columna una, et funiculus duodecim cubitorum circuibat eam : porro grossitudo ejus quatuor digitorum, et intrinsecus cava erat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָעַמּוּדִ֗ים שְׁמֹנֶ֨ה עֶשְׂרֵ֤ה אַמָּה֙ (קומה) [קוֹמַת֙] הָעַמֻּ֣ד הָאֶחָ֔ד וְח֛וּט שְׁתֵּים־עֶשְׂרֵ֥ה אַמָּ֖ה יְסֻבֶּ֑נּוּ וְעׇבְי֛וֹ אַרְבַּ֥ע אֶצְבָּע֖וֹת נָבֽוּב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And concerning the pillars, one pillar was eighteen cubits high: and a cord of twelve cubits compassed it about: but the thickness thereof was four fingers, and it was hollow within.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta patsaat oli kumpikin kahdeksantoistakymmentä kyynärää korkiat, ja kaksitoista kyynärää nuoraa oli mitta hänen ympäriltänsä, ja oli neljän sormenpaksuus, ja avoin sisältä.
verses.king_james: And concerning the pillars, the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits; and a fillet of twelve cubits did compass it; and the thickness thereof was four fingers: it was hollow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et capitella super utramque ærea : altitudo capitelli unius quinque cubitorum, et retiacula et malogranata super coronam in circuitu, omnia ærea : similiter columnæ secundæ, et malogranata.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכֹתֶ֨רֶת עָלָ֜יו נְחֹ֗שֶׁת וְקוֹמַ֨ת הַכֹּתֶ֥רֶת הָאַחַת֮ חָמֵ֣שׁ אַמּוֹת֒ וּשְׂבָכָ֨ה וְרִמּוֹנִ֧ים עַֽל־הַכּוֹתֶ֛רֶת סָבִ֖יב הַכֹּ֣ל נְחֹ֑שֶׁת וְכָאֵ֛לֶּה לַעַמּ֥וּד הַשֵּׁנִ֖י וְרִמּוֹנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And chapiters of brass were upon both: and the height of one chapiter was five cubits: and network, and pomegranates were upon the chapiters round about, all of brass. The same of the second pillar, and the pomegranates.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kruunu sen päällä oli myös vaskesta, joka oli viisi kyynärää korkia, ja verkko ja granatin omenat ympäri kruunun olivat kaikki vaskesta; tainkaltainen oli myös toinen patsas ja granatin omenat.
verses.king_james: And a chapiter of brass was upon it; and the height of one chapiter was five cubits, with network and pomegranates upon the chapiters round about, all of brass. The second pillar also and the pomegranates were like unto these.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fuerunt malogranata nonaginta sex dependentia : et omnia malogranata centum, retiaculis circumdabantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽהְיוּ֙ הָרִמֹּנִ֔ים תִּשְׁעִ֥ים וְשִׁשָּׁ֖ה ר֑וּחָה כׇּל־הָרִמּוֹנִ֥ים מֵאָ֛ה עַל־הַשְּׂבָכָ֖ה סָבִֽיב׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And there were ninety-six pomegranates hanging down: and the pomegranates being a hundred in all, were compassed with network.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja oli sen päällä yhdeksänkymmentä ja kuusi granatin omenaa; kaikki granatin omenat olivat sata yhden verkon päällä aina ympärinsä.
verses.king_james: And there were ninety and six pomegranates on a side; and all the pomegranates upon the network were an hundred round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tulit magister militiæ Saraiam sacerdotem primum, et Sophoniam sacerdotem secundum, et tres custodes vestibuli :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֣ח רַב־טַבָּחִ֗ים אֶת־שְׂרָיָה֙ כֹּהֵ֣ן הָרֹ֔אשׁ וְאֶת־צְפַנְיָ֖ה כֹּהֵ֣ן הַמִּשְׁנֶ֑ה וְאֶת־שְׁלֹ֖שֶׁת שֹׁמְרֵ֥י הַסַּֽף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the general took Saraias the chief priest, and Sophonias the second priest, and the three keepers of the entry.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja huovinhaltia otti papin Serajan ensimäisestä järjestyksestä, ja papin Zephanian toisesta järjestyksestä ja kolme ovenvartiaa.
verses.king_james: And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest, and Zephaniah the second priest, and the three keepers of the door:

verses.latin_vulgate: et de civitate tulit eunuchum unum, qui erat præpositus super viros bellatores : et septem viros de his qui videbant faciem regis, qui inventi sunt in civitate : et scribam principem militum, qui probabat tyrones : et sexaginta viros de populo terræ, qui inventi sunt in medio civitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִן־הָעִ֡יר לָקַח֩ סָרִ֨יס אֶחָ֜ד אֲֽשֶׁר־הָיָ֥ה פָקִ֣יד ׀ עַל־אַנְשֵׁ֣י הַמִּלְחָמָ֗ה וְשִׁבְעָ֨ה אֲנָשִׁ֜ים מֵרֹאֵ֤י פְנֵֽי־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר נִמְצְא֣וּ בָעִ֔יר וְאֵ֗ת סֹפֵר֙ שַׂ֣ר הַצָּבָ֔א הַמַּצְבִּ֖א אֶת־עַ֣ם הָאָ֑רֶץ וְשִׁשִּׁ֥ים אִישׁ֙ מֵעַ֣ם הָאָ֔רֶץ הַֽנִּמְצְאִ֖ים בְּת֥וֹךְ הָעִֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: He also took out of the city one eunuch that was chief over the men of war: and seven men of them that were near the king's person, that were found in the city: and a scribe, an officer of the army who exercised the young soldiers: and threescore men of the people of the land, that were found in the midst of the city.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän otti yhden kamarihaltian kaupungista, joka oli pantu sotaväen päälle, ja seitsemän miestä niistä, jotka aina seisoivat kuninkaan edessä, jotka kaupungissa löydettiin, niin myös joukon ylimmäisen kirjoittajan, joka kirjoitti maan väen, ja kuusikymmentä miestä maan kansasta, jotka kaupungissa löydettiin;
verses.king_james: He took also out of the city an eunuch, which had the charge of the men of war; and seven men of them that were near the king’s person, which were found in the city; and the principal scribe of the host, who mustered the people of the land; and threescore men of the people of the land, that were found in the midst of the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulit autem eos Nabuzardan magister militiæ, et duxit eos ad regem Babylonis in Reblatha :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֣ח אוֹתָ֔ם נְבוּזַרְאֲדָ֖ן רַב־טַבָּחִ֑ים וַיֹּ֧לֶךְ אוֹתָ֛ם אֶל־מֶ֥לֶךְ בָּבֶ֖ל רִבְלָֽתָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Nabuzardan the general took them, and brought them to the king of Babylon, to Reblatha.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nämät otti Nebusaradan, huovinhaltia, ja vei heidät Babelin kuninkaan tykö Riblatiin.
verses.king_james: So Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard took them, and brought them to the king of Babylon to Riblah.

verses.latin_vulgate: et percussit eos rex Babylonis, et interfecit eos in Reblatha in terra Emath : et translatus est Juda de terra sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכֶּ֣ה אוֹתָם֩ מֶ֨לֶךְ בָּבֶ֧ל וַיְמִתֵ֛ם בְּרִבְלָ֖ה בְּאֶ֣רֶץ חֲמָ֑ת וַיִּ֥גֶל יְהוּדָ֖ה מֵעַ֥ל אַדְמָתֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Babylon struck them, and put them to death in Reblatha, in the land of Emath: and Juda was carried away captive out of his land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Babelin kuningas tappoi heidät Riblatissa, Hamatin maalla. Niin Juuda vietiin vankina pois maaltansa.
verses.king_james: And the king of Babylon smote them, and put them to death in Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive out of his own land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iste est populus quem transtulit Nabuchodonosor : in anno septimo, Judæos tria millia et viginti tres :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֣ה הָעָ֔ם אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶגְלָ֖ה נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֑ר בִּשְׁנַת־שֶׁ֕בַע יְהוּדִ֕ים שְׁלֹ֥שֶׁת אֲלָפִ֖ים וְעֶשְׂרִ֥ים וּשְׁלֹשָֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: This is the people whom Nabuchodonosor carried away captive: in the seventh year, three thousand and twenty-three Jews.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on se kansa, jonka Nebukadnetsar vei pois: seitsemäntenä vuonna, kolmetuhatta ja kolme kolmattakymmentä Juudalaista;
verses.king_james: This is the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year three thousand Jews and three and twenty:

verses.latin_vulgate: in anno octavodecimo Nabuchodonosor, de Jerusalem animas octingentas triginta duas :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּשְׁנַ֛ת שְׁמוֹנֶ֥ה עֶשְׂרֵ֖ה לִנְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֑ר מִיר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם נֶ֕פֶשׁ שְׁמֹנֶ֥ה מֵא֖וֹת שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים וּשְׁנָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the eighteenth year of Nabuchodonosor, eight hundred and thirty-two souls from Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Nebukadnetsarin kahdeksantenatoistakymmenentenä vuotena, kahdeksansataa kaksineljättäkymmentä sielua Jerusalemista.
verses.king_james: In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty and two persons:

verses.latin_vulgate: in anno vigesimo tertio Nabuchodonosor, transtulit Nabuzardan magister militiæ animas Judæorum septingentas quadraginta quinque. Omnes ergo animæ, quatuor millia sexcentæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּשְׁנַ֨ת שָׁלֹ֣שׁ וְעֶשְׂרִים֮ לִנְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּר֒ הֶגְלָ֗ה נְבֽוּזַרְאֲדָן֙ רַב־טַבָּחִ֔ים יְהוּדִ֕ים נֶ֕פֶשׁ שְׁבַ֥ע מֵא֖וֹת אַרְבָּעִ֣ים וַחֲמִשָּׁ֑ה כׇּל־נֶ֕פֶשׁ אַרְבַּ֥עַת אֲלָפִ֖ים וְשֵׁ֥שׁ מֵאֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: In the three and twentieth year of Nabuchodonosor, Nabuzardan the general carried away of the Jews seven hundred and forty-five souls. So all the souls were four thousand six hundred.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Nebukadnetsarin kolmantena vuonna kolmattakymmentä vei Nebusaradan, huovinhaltia, pois seitsemänsataa ja viisiviidettäkymmentä sielua Juudasta; kaikki sielut ovat neljätuhatta ja kuusisataa.
verses.king_james: In the three and twentieth year of Nebuchadrezzar Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard carried away captive of the Jews seven hundred forty and five persons: all the persons were four thousand and six hundred.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est in trigesimo septimo anno transmigrationis Joachin regis Juda, duodecimo mense, vigesima quinta mensis, elevavit Evilmerodach rex Babylonis, ipso anno regni sui, caput Joachin regis Juda, et eduxit eum de domo carceris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֩ בִשְׁלֹשִׁ֨ים וָשֶׁ֜בַע שָׁנָ֗ה לְגָלוּת֙ יְהוֹיָכִ֣ן מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֔ה בִּשְׁנֵ֤ים עָשָׂר֙ חֹ֔דֶשׁ בְּעֶשְׂרִ֥ים וַחֲמִשָּׁ֖ה לַחֹ֑דֶשׁ נָשָׂ֡א אֱוִ֣יל מְרֹדַךְ֩ מֶ֨לֶךְ בָּבֶ֜ל בִּשְׁנַ֣ת מַלְכֻת֗וֹ אֶת־רֹאשׁ֙ יְהוֹיָכִ֣ין מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֔ה וַיֹּצֵ֥א אֹת֖וֹ מִבֵּ֥ית (הכליא) [הַכְּלֽוּא]׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the captivity of Joachin king of Juda, in the twelfth month, the five and twentieth day of the month, that Evilmerodach king of Babylon, in the first year of his reign, lifted up the head of Joachin king of Juda, and brought him forth out of prison.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta seitsemäntenä vuonna neljättäkymmentä sen jälkeen kuin Jojakin, Juudan kuningas, oli viety pois, viidentenä päivänä kolmattakymmentä toisessa kuukaudessa toistakymmentä, nosti Evilmerodak, Babelin kuningas, sinä vuonna, jona hän tuli kuninkaaksi, Jojakinin Juudan kuninkaan pään ylös, ja otti hänen vankihuoneesta;
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, in the five and twentieth day of the month, that Evil–merodach king of Babylon in the first year of his reign lifted up the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah, and brought him forth out of prison,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et locutus est cum eo bona, et posuit thronum ejus super thronos regum qui erant post se in Babylone.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְדַבֵּ֥ר אִתּ֖וֹ טֹב֑וֹת וַיִּתֵּן֙ אֶת־כִּסְא֔וֹ מִמַּ֗עַל לְכִסֵּ֧א (מלכים) [הַמְּלָכִ֛ים] אֲשֶׁ֥ר אִתּ֖וֹ בְּבָבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he spoke kindly to him, and he set his throne above the thrones of the kings that were with him in Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja puhui hänen kanssansa ystävällisesti, ja asetti hänen istuimensa ylemmäksi niiden kuningasten istuimia, jotka hänen tykönänsä olivat Babelissa;
verses.king_james: And spake kindly unto him, and set his throne above the throne of the kings that were with him in Babylon,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mutavit vestimenta carceris ejus, et comedebat panem coram eo semper cunctis diebus vitæ suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשִׁנָּ֕ה אֵ֖ת בִּגְדֵ֣י כִלְא֑וֹ וְאָכַ֨ל לֶ֧חֶם לְפָנָ֛יו תָּמִ֖יד כׇּל־יְמֵ֥י חַיָּֽו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he changed his prison garments, and he ate bread before him always all the days of his life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja muutti hänen vankiutensa vaatteet; ja hän söi hänen edessänsä leipää aina kaikkena elinaikanansa.
verses.king_james: And changed his prison garments: and he did continually eat bread before him all the days of his life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cibaria ejus, cibaria perpetua dabantur ei a rege Babylonis, statuta per singulos dies, usque ad diem mortis suæ, cunctis diebus vitæ ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲרֻחָת֗וֹ אֲרֻחַת֩ תָּמִ֨יד נִתְּנָה־לּ֜וֹ מֵאֵ֧ת מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֛ל דְּבַר־י֥וֹם בְּיוֹמ֖וֹ עַד־י֣וֹם מוֹת֑וֹ כֹּ֖ל יְמֵ֥י חַיָּֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And for his diet a continual provision was allowed him by the king of Babylon, every day a portion, until the day of his death, all the days of his life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänelle annettiin alinomaa Babelin kuninkaalta hänen elatuksensa, niinkuin hänelle oli määrätty kaikeksi hänen elinajaksensa, aina hänen kuolemaansa asti.
verses.king_james: And for his diet, there was a continual diet given him of the king of Babylon, every day a portion until the day of his death, all the days of his life.
